vulnerability.title: Install usbguard Package
vulnerability.description: The usbguard package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install usbguard
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( grep -sqE "^.*\.s390x$" /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease || grep -sqE "^s390x$" /proc/sys/kernel/arch; ) && rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ); then if ! rpm -q --quiet "usbguard" ; then dnf install -y "usbguard" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The usbguard package can be installed with the command: $ sudo dnf install usbguard

 1. We must first check if the package is already installed? But the instruction says to install it.
 2. The triage context says: Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms (non-s390x and kernel-core package installed). Then if the usbguard package is not installed, install it.

 However, note that we are only allowed to run one command at a time and we cannot chain.

 We cannot check and then conditionally install in one command because we cannot use conditionals (like if) in a single command without chaining? Actually, we can use a single command that does the check and install? But note: we are to use only one shell command at a time.

 We are allowed to use dnf install which will install the package if not present, and do nothing if already installed.

 So we can simply run: dnf install -y usbguard

 But note: the triage context has a condition for the platform. We are on Rocky Linux 10, which is x86_64 (not s390x) and we assume kernel-core is installed (which it is in a default install). So the condition is satisfied.
